summary: Small GitHub Actions build workflow adjustment in `.github/workflows/build.yml`. The PR moved `ninja diff` so it runs immediately after building and copying the current branch output, before switching to the base/default branch output, and changed the base checkout logic from fetching/switching the repository default branch by name to fetching `origin HEAD` and checking out `FETCH_HEAD`. No PR body, comments, or reviews were present in the slice, so motivation is inferred only from the diff.
what_changed: In the build workflow, `ninja diff` was added after the first `ninja` invocation and after copying `$src` to `$actual`.
The workflow now fetches `origin HEAD` and checks out `FETCH_HEAD` instead of fetching and switching to `${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}`.
The separate `ninja diff` invocation was removed from the `'diff')` case, leaving that case to run `ninja all_source`.
smart_moves: Using `git fetch origin HEAD` plus `git checkout FETCH_HEAD` avoids relying on a local branch name for the repository default branch in CI.
Running `ninja diff` before checking out the comparison branch keeps the diff step tied to the PR/head build state rather than after the workflow has switched revisions.
The change is deliberately minimal: only three added and three deleted lines in the workflow.
decomp_lessons: For decomp CI, keep build/diff validation order explicit when the workflow switches between PR and base revisions.
Prefer detached checkout of a fetched ref such as `FETCH_HEAD` when the CI job only needs to build a comparison revision and does not need a named local branch.
Small CI ordering changes can affect whether generated progress or diff artifacts correspond to the PR revision or the baseline revision.
follow_up_queries: Find CI workflow PRs that modify `ninja diff` ordering.
Find PRs that change GitHub Actions checkout or fetch behavior.
Find build.yml changes involving `FETCH_HEAD` or repository default branch handling.
